channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Minister Raphael Tuju Receives Medical Care While in Police Custody at Karen Station
item.description: Former Cabinet Minister Raphael Tuju, a well-known political figure in Kenya, was treated by doctors from Karen Hospital while being held at the Karen Police Station on Monday. He reportedly complaine...

item.title: Kim Jong-un Declares North Korea’s Nuclear Status Permanent and Warns South Korea
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un stated that North Korea will continue to strengthen its nuclear forces permanently, designating South Korea as its most hostile neighbor. In a speech to the Supreme Peo...

item.title: Confusion surrounds Trump’s claims of US-Iran talks amid ongoing Middle East conflict
item.description: In Washington, global markets reacted sharply after US President Donald Trump announced a temporary halt to planned military strikes on Iran, citing what he called 'productive' discussions between Was...

item.title: Ontario hospitals warn of deep financial challenges as deficits and staffing cuts mount
item.description: Ontario’s hospitals are facing severe financial strain, with more than 100 institutions projecting year-end deficits despite balanced-budget requirements. The Ontario Hospital Association (OHA) warns ...

item.title: Canberra Liberals respond to rising One Nation support ahead of next election
item.description: The Canberra Liberals, led by Mark Parton, are preparing to counter the growing influence of One Nation following the latter’s strong showing in South Australia. The recent state election saw the Libe...

item.title: Karnataka government proposes guidelines to limit mobile and social media use for children under 16
item.description: The Karnataka government has announced plans to regulate mobile phone and social media use among children under the age of 16. Education Minister Madhu Bangarappa stated in the Legislative Council tha...

item.title: FCC blocks new foreign-made routers over cybersecurity risks
item.description: The United States Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has announced a new order banning the import of all newly manufactured foreign-made consumer routers due to cybersecurity threats, particularl...

item.title: US journalists challenge administration over political influence on Voice of America
item.description: Journalists working for the US government-funded broadcaster Voice of America (VOA) have taken legal action against the Trump administration, alleging that the outlet’s independence has been compromis...

item.title: Housing reform and land release: Key steps after SONA 2026
item.description: In his 2026 State of the Nation Address, President Cyril Ramaphosa laid out more concrete steps to tackle South Africa's longstanding housing crisis. His plan includes accelerating the release of well...

item.title: TECH Global University: Leading Global Online Education with 14,000 Programs
item.description: TECH Global University ni di world’s largest online university, offering high-quality education fully online in 11 languages across 150 countries. The university get over 14,000 degree programs coveri...

item.title: Thika Student Builds Home for His Father Using Savings from Watermelon Farming
item.description: A young Kenyan college student named Shalmah has warmed hearts after building a beautiful house for his father in Busia County. The 22-year-old, currently studying at JFC Munene College of Health Scie...

item.title: Court Overturns Rape Conviction Due to Legal Technicality
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Tanzania has overturned a 30-year prison sentence for Lemindea Lesira in a rape case, citing a significant legal error. The initial conviction, handed down by a lower court, als...

item.title: India to Finalise Rafale, Transport Aircraft, and AEW&C Deals in 2026-27 to Modernise IAF
item.description: The Indian Defence Ministry has informed the parliamentary standing committee on defence that major procurement deals are scheduled for conclusion in the financial year 2026-27 to enhance the Indian A...

item.title: Key Candidates for Assam 2026 Elections: Himanta Sarma from Jalukbari and Pradyut Bordoloi from Dispur
item.description: The upcoming Assam Legislative Assembly elections in 2026 are beginning to take shape as key candidates announce their constituencies. Himanta Biswa Sarma, the current Chief Minister of Assam, has dec...

item.title: PSL 11 to Begin in Lahore and Karachi Amid Fuel Crisis and Security Measures
item.description: LAHORE: The 11th edition of the HBL Pakistan Super League (PSL) is all set to start from March 26, 2026, despite the unusual challenges facing the country. Several foreign players have already arrived...

item.title: U.S. Judge Alvin Hellerstein to Oversee Nicolas Maduro's Drug Trafficking Case
item.description: Alvin Hellerstein, a 92-year-old U.S. federal judge, is set to preside over the drug trafficking trial of former Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro. The trial, expected to be lengthy, has already see...

item.title: Stanbic Bank Tanzania wins three global private banking awards in 2026
item.description: Stanbic Bank Tanzania has once again made headlines by winning three prestigious global awards at the Euromoney Private Banking Awards 2026 held in London. For the second year in a row, the bank was n...

item.title: PTA grants technical approval for Telenor–Ufone merger as legal process begins
item.description: The Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (PTA) has formally approved the technical merger of Telenor Pakistan and Ufone. This process will now be communicated to the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX), as ...

item.title: Police Intensify Investigations Into Suspected Mass Grave in Kericho as Witness Search Continues
item.description: Police in Kenya have stepped up investigations following reports of a suspected mass grave discovered at Makaburini Cemetery in Kericho County. According to the National Police Service (NPS), officers...

item.title: Analysis of US Military Struggles Against Iran
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing military tensions between the United States and Iran, highlighting significant challenges faced by US forces. According to the report, Iran has successfully destroyed...
